class Solution {
public:
    int countDigitOne(int n) {
        long long d = 1;
        int result = 0;

            d *= 10;

        return result;

};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int countDigitOne(int n) {
        long long d = 1;
        int res = 0;
        while (d <= n) {
            long long high = n / (d * 10);
            int current = (n / d) % 10;
            long long low = n % d;
            res += high * d;
            if (current > 1) {
                res += d;
            } else if (current == 1) {
                res += low + 1;
            }
            d *= 10;
        }
        return res;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a digit-by-digit analysis to calculate the number of 1s in each position. For each digit position, it computes contributions based on higher digits, current digit, and lower digits. This avoids iterating through all numbers up to n, achieving logarithmic time complexity. The approach is efficient and handles large n values effectively.
